content.problemStatement: Print the number 108108108 using arithmetic operations on the numbers 999 and 121212.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. No further input is required for each test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the integer 108 on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case, so the program prints 108 once.
Three test cases require three lines, each containing 108.
Five test cases produce five lines of 108.

solution.approach: The required value 108 can be obtained by multiplying 9 and 12 (9 * 12 = 108). Since the problem does not depend on any varying input, the answer is constant. Read the number of test cases T and print 108 exactly T times, each on a new line.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
